refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall establish and maintain a documented performance requirements baseline derived from worksite mission context, including cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability targets, with full traceability to worksite inputs and a managed history of all baseline and calibration updates.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct concerns: derivation (how targets are computed), recording (data capture), and management (lifecycle control). Refinement separates these into a coherent functional statement that emphasizes the baseline as a managed artifact with traceability and history. This aligns with the acceptance criterion requiring documented, traceable performance targets and baseline/calibration history.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) performance targets are derived from documented worksite mission inputs; (2) a baseline record exists with timestamp, source inputs, and derivation method; (3) historical records of all baseline updates and calibration changes are maintained with audit trail; (4) traceability links from each target back to originating worksite input are documented and queryable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record and store the performance requirements baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source inputs, derivation method, owner).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a complete history of baseline versions and calibration updates with audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Establish and maintain traceability links from each performance target to originating worksite input.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance requirements management system (PRMS) that ingests worksite mission context via structured input forms, applies derivation rules (algorithmic or rule-based), stores baseline snapshots with full metadata, and maintains versioned history with audit logging.

DP-B: Distributed performance target derivation where each vehicle/subsystem independently computes targets from received mission context, with periodic synchronization to a central baseline registry that records snapshots and history.

DP-C: Manual performance requirements specification by Site Manager with system-assisted validation and traceability, supported by a document management system that tracks versions and calibration updates.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance requirements management system (PRMS) that ingests worksite mission context via structured input forms, applies derivation rules (algorithmic or rule-based), stores baseline snapshots with full metadata, and maintains versioned history with audit logging.

potential_sources: PRMS must receive worksite mission context inputs (coupled to worksite planning/tasking domain).
PRMS must output performance targets to vehicle control and teleoperation subsystems (coupled to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules).
PRMS must integrate safety constraints from Safety Services (coupled to safety domain).
PRMS baseline updates must be communicated to all dependent systems via Tasking interface.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is acceptable and expected at system boundaries. DP-A centralizes the derivation logic, reducing algorithmic diffusion and enabling consistent baseline management. Decoupling is achieved via well-defined interfaces (Tasking for target distribution, Telemetry for mission context feedback).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context can be captured in structured form (e.g., JSON, database schema) without loss of critical information.
Assumption: Derivation rules are deterministic and can be codified (either algorithmic or rule-based engine).
Risk: If worksite inputs are highly unstructured or context-dependent, derivation may require human judgment, reducing automation and introducing variability.
Risk: Baseline updates may conflict with in-flight operations; change management and version control are critical.
Risk: High safety criticality requires that all baseline changes are validated and approved before deployment.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the derivation rule set (algorithmic vs. rule-based vs. hybrid) and define the approval/validation workflow for baseline updates, especially for safety-critical targets. Establish a formal change control process that includes Safety Manager review for high-criticality updates.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the system-level container responsible for integrating all subsystems and managing cross-cutting concerns including performance requirements baseline management, system-wide configuration, and mission-level orchestration. The PRMS (Performance Requirements Management System) is a logical subsystem within NGHE Solution that owns the derivation, recording, and lifecycle management of performance targets.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: PRMS receives worksite mission context and operational parameters via Tasking (inbound); PRMS outputs derived performance targets and baseline updates to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems via Tasking (outbound).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: PRMS receives operational telemetry (actual cycle times, energy consumption, availability metrics) from Vehicle and Teleoperation to validate baseline assumptions and inform calibration updates.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: PRMS receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services (inbound); PRMS outputs performance targets that incorporate safety margins and constraints (outbound).

module_containment_scores.notes: PRMS is a core system-level function that must integrate mission context, safety constraints, and performance targets across all subsystems. Containment within NGHE Solution is appropriate because: (1) performance requirements baseline is a system-wide artifact, (2) derivation rules are system-level policy, (3) baseline management requires cross-subsystem coordination. Minimal diffusion risk: PRMS logic is centralized; only interface contracts (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) are distributed.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing performance targets, not deriving them. Placing PRMS in Vehicle would create high diffusion: (1) performance targets are mission-level, not vehicle-specific, (2) multiple vehicles may operate under the same baseline, (3) derivation logic would be duplicated across vehicle instances, (4) baseline history and audit trail would be fragmented.

Teleoperation is a control mode, not a requirements management system. Placing PRMS in Teleoperation would create severe diffusion: (1) performance targets apply to both autonomous and teleoperated modes, (2) baseline management would be mode-specific rather than system-wide, (3) tele-operator would have inappropriate authority over system-level requirements, (4) non-teleoperated operations would lack baseline management.

Safety Services provides safety constraints and validation, but does not own performance requirements derivation. Placing PRMS in Safety Services would create diffusion: (1) performance targets include non-safety metrics (cycle time, energy efficiency), (2) Safety Manager should validate but not own baseline management, (3) safety constraints are inputs to PRMS, not the reverse, (4) separation of concerns is violated.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (System Engineering / Project Management authority)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The PRMS (Performance Requirements Management System) is logically contained within NGHE Solution as a system-level service. Physically, PRMS may be realized as a centralized software service (e.g., cloud-based or on-premises database with derivation engine and API) that is accessed by Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules via well-defined interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert). This architecture ensures: (1) single source of truth for performance baselines, (2) centralized audit trail and version control, (3) consistent derivation rules across all operations, (4) clear ownership and change control authority.
containment_risks: Risk: If PRMS is realized as a centralized service, network latency or service unavailability could delay baseline updates or target distribution. Mitigation: Implement caching and fallback mechanisms in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Risk: PRMS may become a bottleneck for performance target updates during high-frequency mission changes. Mitigation: Design PRMS with scalable architecture (e.g., microservice, load-balanced API).

Risk: Tight coupling between PRMS and Safety Services could create circular dependencies if safety constraints are derived from performance targets. Mitigation: Establish clear input/output contracts; Safety Services provides constraints (inputs), PRMS incorporates them (processing), outputs validated targets.

Risk: Worksite mission context may be provided by external systems (e.g., Site Manager planning tool) with varying data quality or format. Mitigation: Implement data validation and schema enforcement at PRMS ingestion point.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define the physical realization of PRMS (centralized service vs. distributed components) and specify the API contracts for Tasking, Telemetry, and SafetyAlert interfaces. Establish a formal change control process for baseline updates that includes approval gates (System Engineer, Safety Manager, Project Manager). Document the fallback strategy if PRMS service is unavailable.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized PRMS) directly addresses all aspects of the refined FR: (1) derives performance targets from worksite mission context via structured input processing, (2) records baselines with full metadata (timestamp, source, method), (3) maintains versioned history with audit logging, (4) establishes traceability via metadata links. The DP is complete, feasible, and testable. No decoupling issues identified.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk (Low): PRMS logic is centralized in NGHE Solution, but interface contracts (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) are distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. This is acceptable because interfaces are well-defined and stable.

Diffusion Risk (Low): Baseline updates must be propagated to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Mitigation: Use Tasking interface as the authoritative channel for target distribution; implement version tracking to detect stale targets.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: PRMS is appropriately contained within NGHE Solution as a system-level service. No algorithmic or data diffusion is expected. Interface diffusion is minimal and managed via contract-based communication. Ownership is clear: System Engineer / Project Manager authority over baseline management.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible and traceable: (1) Derive performance targets from documented worksite inputs and confirm targets match derivation rules. (2) Inspect baseline records for required metadata (timestamp, source, method, owner). (3) Query baseline history and confirm all updates are logged with audit trail. (4) Trace each target back to originating worksite input via metadata links. (5) Validate that baseline updates are approved and change-controlled. Acceptance criteria are met: documented, traceable performance targets with managed history.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: PRMS (within NGHE Solution) owns the derivation, recording, and management of performance baselines. Vehicle and Teleoperation modules consume targets via Tasking interface. Safety Services provides constraints via SafetyAlert interface. Telemetry interface provides feedback for calibration. No ambiguity in responsibility or interface ownership.

recommended_revisions: Formalize the derivation rule set (algorithmic, rule-based, or hybrid) and document the rule repository as part of PRMS specification.

Define the approval workflow for baseline updates: who approves (System Engineer, Safety Manager, Project Manager), what criteria are evaluated (safety, feasibility, impact), and what documentation is required.
Specify the data schema for worksite mission context inputs and performance targets to ensure consistent interpretation across all modules.
Establish a baseline versioning and rollback strategy to handle failed deployments or conflicting updates.
Define the audit trail format and retention policy for baseline history (e.g., immutable log, database with change tracking).

decision.justification: The refined FR is not atomic; it comprises four distinct sub-FRs (derivation, recording, history management, traceability). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define specific DPs and IMs. The selected DP-A (Centralized PRMS) is sound at the system level, but implementation details (derivation rules, data schemas, approval workflows) must be resolved at the next decomposition level. The FR-DP-IM gates all pass, indicating readiness to decompose sub-FRs. High safety criticality requires detailed specification of safety-critical sub-functions (e.g., safety constraint integration, baseline validation).
decision.justification_detail: CR-NGHE-005 is a meta-requirement that orchestrates multiple functional concerns. Decomposing into sub-FRs allows each concern to be addressed with appropriate rigor and traceability. Sub-FRs 005.1 (derivation) and 005.4 (traceability) are particularly critical for safety and require detailed DP specification. Sub-FRs 005.2 (recording) and 005.3 (history) are data management concerns that require schema and lifecycle specification.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context inputs. [Requires DP: Derivation rule engine with input validation and constraint integration]
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record and store the performance requirements baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source inputs, derivation method, owner). [Requires DP: Baseline data model and storage schema]
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a complete history of baseline versions and calibration updates with audit trail. [Requires DP: Version control and immutable audit log]
CR-NGHE-005.4: Establish and maintain traceability links from each performance target to originating worksite input. [Requires DP: Traceability metadata model and query interface]

assumptions: Worksite mission context can be captured in structured form (e.g., JSON, database schema).
Derivation rules are deterministic and can be codified (algorithmic or rule-based).
Performance targets are system-level artifacts, not vehicle-specific or mode-specific.
Baseline updates are infrequent relative to operational cycles, allowing for change control and approval workflows.
Safety constraints are provided by Safety Services and are inputs to PRMS, not outputs.
Telemetry feedback is available for baseline validation and calibration.
open_risks: Risk: Unstructured or context-dependent worksite inputs may require human judgment, reducing automation.
Risk: Baseline updates may conflict with in-flight operations; change management is critical.
Risk: High safety criticality requires rigorous validation and approval of baseline changes.
Risk: PRMS service availability and performance could impact mission planning and execution.
Risk: Data quality and consistency of worksite mission context inputs may vary across sites.
evidence_needed: Specification of worksite mission context data model and input schema.
Derivation rule set (algorithmic, rule-based, or hybrid) with examples and validation criteria.
Baseline data model with required metadata fields (timestamp, source, method, owner, approval status).
Version control and audit trail design (immutable log, database schema, retention policy).
Traceability metadata model linking targets to source inputs.
Change control workflow and approval authority (System Engineer, Safety Manager, Project Manager).
API contracts for Tasking, Telemetry, and SafetyAlert interfaces.
Fallback and recovery strategy if PRMS service is unavailable.
Test cases for derivation rules, baseline recording, history management, and traceability.
